objects.name: Analytic 1701
objects.description:
Correlates (1) activation of Device Administrator privileges by an application, (2) absence or mismatch of legitimate user interaction during the approval flow, and (3) immediate execution of administrator-level control actions (e.g., password reset, device lock, policy enforcement, prevention of uninstall). The defender observes a causal chain where an application transitions into a privileged device control role and rapidly exercises those capabilities outside expected user-driven patterns.

Application vetting services can check for the string `BIND_DEVICE_ADMIN` in the application’s manifest.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application invokes DevicePolicyManager APIs (e.g., resetPassword, lockNow, setCameraDisabled) immediately following admin activation

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application granted Device Administrator privilege + abnormal activation pattern (e.g., rapid enablement after install or no recent user interaction)

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Defines correlation window between Device Admin activation and subsequent privileged actions
Baseline of legitimate applications expected to request Device Administrator privileges (e.g., enterprise MDM agents)
Defines acceptable timing between user interaction and admin activation
List of high-risk DevicePolicyManager API actions monitored for abuse
